creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text:
The article isn't inaccurate. "Atomic Time Scales" doesn't mean TAI (which is an unadjusted atomic time scale. UTC is also an atomic time scale derived from TAI with the addition of leap seconds. It's perfectly accurate as written. The very definition of a UTC is
UTC = TAI - leapseconds.
And the value is published in bulletin A every 6 months.

And the 'on UTC' is not how the time keeping business describes it. A more accurate phrase than "adjustment made on UTC" would be "adjustment made to UTC".

Even better would be:

A leap second is a one second adjustment made at specific times of year to UTC to adjust the atomic time scale for variations in the rotation of the earth.
